commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows` planner/catalog, rewires live transport standard scenario planning through it, and updates package/docs/API-baseline metadata and tests.

PR surface: Source +724, Tests +224, Docs +1, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +957 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/API PR rather than a reported bug. Source inspection shows current main lacks the new `qa-user-flows` planner, and the PR adds focused planner tests.

target.title: Add live transport scenario planning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows`, a shared user-flow catalog/planner with surfaces, capability atoms, optional owner mappings, running-gateway execution metadata, and selected/skipped plan output.
- Reworks live transport standard scenarios to sit on top of the generic user-flow planner and maps channel cases like canary, mention gating, allowlist block, threads, reactions, restart, and help to user-flow metadata.
- Exposes the planner through QA runtime, package exports, SDK docs metadata, and the plugin SDK API baseline so QA Lab/Kova can build on the same contract instead of inventing a parallel JSON language.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-live-transport-scenarios.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts --threads=8`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw now has a shared user-flow planning contract that can select runnable flows and explain skipped flows from capabilities, driver support, and requested ids.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-user-flows`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-live-transport-scenarios.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-user-flows.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts --threads=8`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`; `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: The planner tests pass for optional mappings, requested-flow filtering, live-transport-backed flows, unknown id validation, and selected/skipped output; SDK export and API baseline checks pass.
Observed result after fix: User-flow plans carry running-gateway execution metadata and report selected flows plus explicit skip reasons for missing capabilities, missing driver support, and request filtering.
What was not tested: No live Telegram/Slack/etc. lane was run; this PR adds the shared mapping/planning contract and keeps execution on the existing QA Lab/live transport runners.
target.author: romneyda
